svg.text: TIGRINYA — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
SERIES 34D OF 60 · REMEMBER FORWARD
— PASSAGE 1 — ERITREAN PROVERB —
የዐ ቙ቱ ወደ ዙሃ ይትኖኘ ኡለም።
yəha qɨtɨ wədə zɨhəa yətnəbib aləm.
The person who goes toward the light does not stumble.
"The one who walks toward the light does not stumble."
— PASSAGE 2 — TIGRINYA PROVERB —
ዘይን ዐይን እንተው ኪትክለተ ካዓይ ብዘዓ ኣተዋ።
That which you do not speak, even the wisest person does not know.
"What you do not say, even the wise do not know." — Speak your needs.
— BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
ይህ መሰዘቲ ገይርነ ብጣዘከት ብወጨ ዘየመፀደመታ ዘኰካዱ ።
Yəh məsəzətɨ gəyrənə bɨtatəzəkət bɨwəqɨ zəyəməqədəməta zəwəkadɨ.
This message for-you freely by-those who-the-future remembered.
"This message was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN TIGRINYA —
መፀደመታ (məqədəməta): "future / ahead" — from qədəmə (to precede / to go before).
Literally "the preceding-ness" — the future as what stands ahead and goes before us.
ዘኰካዱ (zəwəkadɨ): "remembered" — from root w-k-d / z-k-r (to remember, to be mindful).
ብወጨ (bɨwəqɨ): "freely / for free" — literally "without price".
— DECODING NOTE — FOR FUTURE READERS —
Tigrinya is the official language of Eritrea and the dominant language of the Tigray region of Ethiopia.
~10 million speakers total. Written in the Ethiopic (Fidel) script — same as Amharic (Plate 31A).
Tigrinya is a Semitic language (Afroasiatic) — related to Arabic, Amharic, Hebrew, and Ge'ez.
Ge'ez (Classical Ethiopic) is the liturgical ancestor of Tigrinya, still used in church services.
SERIES 34 · TIGRINYA · PLATE D · TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
